question: What is the refueling interval for the NuScale reactor?

nuscale_ch01_0036.title: Refueling Operations
nuscale_ch01_0036.summary: Refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM) are conducted independently from other modules. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling pool, disassembling it for inspection, maintenance, and refueling, and then reassembling and returning it to service after successful testing and startup procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0438.title: SRP 10.4.6, Rev 3: Condensate Cleanup SystemII.1 Maintain direct cycle BWR plant water quality to avoid corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (GDC 14)
nuscale_ch01_0438.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for systems related to condensate cleanup and associated components. It notes that certain requirements are not applicable to NuScale’s design (such as those specific to BWRs), while for PWR water quality, the design conforms but with differences in secondary chemistry requirements due to the unique configuration of the NuScale steam generator. Overall, the document details compliance or justified departures from standard criteria for each system.

nuscale_ch01_0309.title: SRP 9.1.4, Rev 4: Light Load Handling System and Refueling Cavity DesignII.3 Specific Criteria to Meet GDC 61
nuscale_ch01_0309.summary: The section documents NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Light Load Handling System and Refueling Cavity Design, specifically addressing compliance with General Design Criterion (GDC) 61 as outlined in SRP 9.1.4, Rev 4. It confirms that the design meets the required criteria, with no exceptions or additional comments noted.

nuscale_ch01_0018.title: Principal Design Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0018.summary: The section outlines the principal design criteria for the NuScale plant, describing the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) and the overall facility process. It explains the reactor core's heat transfer process, the natural circulation of coolant, and the generation of superheated steam to drive a turbine and produce electricity, with heat ultimately removed via a shared circulating water loop and condenser.

nuscale_ch01_0439.title: SRP 10.4.6, Rev 3: Condensate Cleanup SystemII.2 Maintain indirect cycle PWR water quality to avoid corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (GDC 14)
nuscale_ch01_0439.summary: The section outlines NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for maintaining indirect cycle PWR water quality to prevent corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, as required by GDC 14. It notes that in the NuScale design, primary water is outside the steam generator tubes and secondary water is inside, with no steam generator blowdown, resulting in secondary chemistry requirements that differ from standard guidance.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0054.title: Requirements for Additional Technical Information
nuscale_ch01_0054.summary: This section outlines the verification and confirmation testing programs for unique design features of the NuScale Power Plant, which provide essential data to support the final safety analyses. It details completed and ongoing tests for the NuScale Power Module, focusing on areas where prior data or operational experience was lacking, including specific tests for fuel design, steam generator, control rod assemblies, and integrated system phenomena. The section also describes critical heat flux testing for both preliminary and final fuel designs to meet licensing requirements and support safety analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0056.title: Critical Heat Flux Testing - Preliminary Fuel Design
nuscale_ch01_0056.summary: This section outlines the verification and confirmation tests conducted to support the safety analysis of the NuScale Power Plant, focusing on unique design features lacking prior data or operational experience. Specifically, it describes two critical heat flux (CHF) test programs for the NuScale fuel design—one for the preliminary design, which aided code development and safety analysis, and another for the final design. The preliminary CHF testing provided essential data for developing the NSP2 CHF correlation used in the final fuel design.

nuscale_ch01_0055.title: NuScale Testing Programs
nuscale_ch01_0055.summary: This section outlines the NuScale testing programs designed to verify and confirm unique design features supporting the plant’s safety analysis, particularly where prior data or operational experience was lacking. It summarizes completed and ongoing tests for the NuScale Power Module, including those for fuel design, steam generator, control rod assemblies, and integrated system phenomena, with specific mention of critical heat flux testing for both preliminary and final fuel designs to support licensing and safety analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0121.title: 1.9 Conformance with Regulatory Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0121.summary: This section outlines how the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report demonstrates compliance with applicable regulatory criteria, using a series of tables to assess conformance with various NRC regulations and guidance documents effective six months prior to the anticipated docket date. It details the evaluation process, including identification of deviations and justifications for alternative approaches, and specifies that future applicants must similarly address regulatory conformance for site-specific and operational aspects.

nuscale_ch01_0684.title: DSRS 15.1.5, Rev 0: Steam System Piping Failures Inside and Outside of ContainmentII.6 AssumptionsCore Burnup
nuscale_ch01_0684.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for steam system piping failures inside and outside containment, specifically addressing assumptions such as core burnup, rod withdrawal, core flow, and postulated failures of main steam lines. It notes that NuScale’s analysis generally conforms to DSRS 15.1.5 requirements, with some exceptions and clarifications, such as using critical heat flux for plant phenomena and referencing SRP Section 15.0.3 for radiological analyses. Operator action is not required for mitigation, and nonradiological analysis provisions remain applicable.

nuscale_ch01_0074.title: Conformance with Regulatory Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0074.summary: This section outlines how the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report demonstrates compliance with relevant regulatory criteria, using a series of tables to assess conformance with various NRC regulations and guidance documents effective six months prior to the anticipated docket date. It details the evaluation process, including identification of deviations and justifications for alternative approaches, and specifies that future applicants referencing the NuScale design must similarly address regulatory conformance for site-specific and operational aspects.

nuscale_ch01_0466.title: DSRS 11.1, Rev 0: Coolant Source TermsII.13 The design basis coolant source term is based on a combination of assumptions of failed fuel fractions
nuscale_ch01_0466.summary: The design basis coolant source term for the NuScale reactor is partially based on a failed fuel fraction significantly less than 0.25%, as detailed in NuScale’s technical report. While the NuScale design uses an alternate methodology for effluent release calculations due to differences from large PWRs, it generally conforms to regulatory criteria, with some partial conformance noted where site-specific analyses are required.

nuscale_ch01_0287.title: SRP BTP 8-8, (Feb 2012): Onsite (Emergency Diesel Generators) and Offsite Power Sources Allowed Outage Time ExtensionsAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0287.summary: The section explains that NuScale’s design does not rely on AC power for safety-related functions, making certain regulatory outage time requirements for emergency diesel generators and offsite power sources inapplicable. It also summarizes the plant’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for electrical systems and fuel storage, noting areas of full or partial compliance and referencing relevant sections for details.

nuscale_ch01_0470.title: DSRS 11.2, Rev 0: Liquid Waste Management SystemII.3 Seismic Design of Structures Housing Liquid Waste Management System Components
nuscale_ch01_0470.summary: This section outlines the NuScale design’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the seismic design of structures housing liquid waste management system components. It states that the design conforms to the relevant requirements, with no exceptions or additional notes provided.

nuscale_ch01_0294.title: DSRS 9.1.2, Rev 0: New and Spent Fuel StorageII.3 Specific Criteria to Meet GDC 5
nuscale_ch01_0294.summary: The section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for new and spent fuel storage, specifically addressing the requirements of DSRS 9.1.2, Rev 0, Section II.3, which pertains to meeting General Design Criterion (GDC) 5. It states that NuScale fully conforms to the specific criteria for GDC 5, with no exceptions or additional requirements noted, and references supporting sections 9.1.2.1 and 9.1.2.3 for further details.

nuscale_ch01_0700.title: DSRS 15.1.6, Rev 0: Loss of Containment VacuumII.3 Analytical ParametersCore Burnup
nuscale_ch01_0700.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for DSRS 15.1.6, specifically regarding analytical parameters such as core burnup, initial power level, and conservative scram, all of which are confirmed to conform with requirements. It also notes that for related events in DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5, most criteria are met except for the steam pressure regulator, which is not present in the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It provides a side-by-side summary of key characteristics such as electrical output, core design, reactor coolant system, and safety components, highlighting significant differences in size, complexity, and safety system requirements between NuScale and conventional PWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0050.title: Diesel Generator Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0050.summary: The Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs) in the NuScale Power Plant each house a single backup diesel generator and their auxiliary equipment, serving as support structures without any safety-related systems or functions supporting engineered safety features (ESFs). The DGBs contain diesel engines, generators, HVAC systems, and maintenance areas, and are not required for plant safety operations.
